chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special Customs Reference Application No. 610 of 2022 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection No. 25. 2. For order on CMA No. 3379/22. 3. For hearing of main case. 4. For order on CMA No. 3380/22. 30.10.2025 Mr. Muhammad Khalil Dogar, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel files statement, which is taken on record. Same is reproduced herein below:- “STATEMENT ON BEHALF OF THE APPELLANT (APPRAISEMENT-WEST) 1. That the Applicant had filed the Special Customs Reference Application No. 610/2022 challenging the judgment in Customs Appeal No. K-770/2022 dated 29.06.2022 passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal. 2. That upon a detailed examination of the record and the impugned judgment, the Applicant respectfully submits that no substantial question of law arises warranting consideration. 3. That the Applicant, therefore, does not wish to press the present SCRA and seeks permission to withdraw the same. 4. That the withdrawal of the petition is bona fide and without prejudice to the Applicant’s rights and remedies available under law, if any. 5. The aforesaid compliance report is filed for consideration of this Honoruable Court of in the interest of justice. Karachi: Sd/- Dated:29.10.2025. (Deputy Collector of Customs) Appraisement-West (Group-V)” In view hereof, the reference application is dismissed as withdrawn with listed applications. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969.

Karachi: Sd/- Dated:29.10.2025. (Deputy Collector of Customs) Appraisement-West (Group-V)” In view hereof, the reference application is dismissed as withdrawn with listed applications. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge Ayaz p.s.
